definition_long: Tag indicating presence of Scandinavian in the scene or data. Apply when the overall design language aligns clearly with Scandinavian. Do not assign based on a single object or minor accent.
Tag indicating presence of Traditional in the scene or data. Apply when the overall design language aligns clearly with Traditional. Do not assign based on a single object or minor accent.
Tag indicating presence of Evidence Pointer in the scene or data. Use when this attribute is clearly present and salient in the scene or data. Do not infer without evidence.
Tag indicating presence of Request Source in the scene or data. Use when this attribute is clearly present and salient in the scene or data. Do not infer without evidence.
Tag indicating presence of Contrast in the scene or data. Use when this attribute is clearly present and salient in the scene or data. Do not infer without evidence.
Tag indicating presence of Homogeneity in the scene or data. Use when this attribute is clearly present and salient in the scene or data. Do not infer without evidence.
Tag indicating presence of Contrast in the scene or data. Use when this attribute is clearly present and salient in the scene or data. Do not infer without evidence.
Tag indicating presence of Homogeneity in the scene or data. Use when this attribute is clearly present and salient in the scene or data. Do not infer without evidence.

Perceived affective response described as Cozy in the observer's experience of the environment. Use when observer reports or validated instruments indicate this affective/cognitive response. Avoid projecting the response from style cues alone.

Perceived affective response described as Jarring in the observer's experience of the environment. Use when observer reports or validated instruments indicate this affective/cognitive response. Avoid projecting the response from style cues alone.

Perceived affective response described as Scary in the observer's experience of the environment. Use when observer reports or validated instruments indicate this affective/cognitive response. Avoid projecting the response from style cues alone.

Perceived affective response described as Tranquil in the observer's experience of the environment. Use when observer reports or validated instruments indicate this affective/cognitive response. Avoid projecting the response from style cues alone.

Perceived affective response described as Welcoming in the observer's experience of the environment. Use when observer reports or validated instruments indicate this affective/cognitive response. Avoid projecting the response from style cues alone.

Observable environmental attribute where Illuminance level (task surface) is present. This is a computed or measured metric from environmental, spatial, or visual data; use only when the metric is calculated for the scene. Do not guess the value from appearance.
